required_inputs: request intent and requested technician support action
technician support specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
clinical, payer, privacy, warranty, service-contract, safety, field-engineering, financial, and legal authority indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer interpretation, coverage approval, authorization approval, coding, claim payment, privacy disclosure approval, warranty adjudication, or policy exception
request asks the lane to authorize unsafe device use, field repair method, substitute part, licensed trade work, hazardous handling, or safety-critical release
request asks to bypass service, warranty, authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, or PHI prerequisites

required_inputs: authoritative eCFR, CMS, HHS OCR, FDA, CAQH CORE, ASTP/ONC, and FTC source set
source freshness, refresh interval, and decay metadata
tenant policy, payer rule, manufacturer or dealer program, warranty, service-contract, and jurisdiction overlays
required_outputs: source or policy conflict escalation note
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing or stale for a controlling disposition
public guidance, payer rule, tenant policy, manufacturer rule, warranty term, service-contract term, or jurisdiction materially conflicts
source context would change PHI handling, payer interpretation, clinical risk, safety posture, or warranty exception handling

required_inputs: work order, service ticket, dispatch, technician note, equipment, device, model, serial, warranty, part, CRM, and complaint records
EHR/EMR, order, encounter, authorization, referral, eligibility, payer portal, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, privacy, and audit records when healthcare linked
source hierarchy, record links, timestamps, owner state, and unresolved blockers
refusal_triggers: critical technician support artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from equipment state, warranty posture, coverage dependency, owner, and next action cannot be established
customer or stakeholder summary would overstate what the records prove

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across work-order, dispatch, field-service, technician, warranty, dealer, manufacturer, CRM, EHR/EMR, payer portal, authorization, referral, eligibility, revenue cycle, privacy, and audit systems
tenant routing matrix, RACI, source hierarchy, service model, escalation SLA, retrieval hooks, and prerequisite rules
equipment identity, warranty or service-contract posture, part state, authorization, referral, eligibility, clinical, payer, privacy, safety, and technician dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner or retrieval hook is unresolved

systems of record materially disagree on work-order state, equipment identity, warranty status, part availability, authorization, referral, eligibility, claim state, PHI posture, technician note, or closure state
required tenant source hierarchy or routing matrix is unavailable

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient-safety, PHI, payer, warranty, part, technician, and system-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts, records, source hierarchy, or authority maps would change the technician support disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, warranty exception, field safety, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis
confidence is too low to close, communicate, or route the case as resolved

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
minimum-necessary evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, licensed clinician, patient access, utilization management, medical coding, revenue cycle, billing, payer operations, healthcare privacy, compliance, dispatch, warranty adjudication, legal, finance, field engineering, licensed trade, or patient safety ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff requires PHI or clinical context outside minimum-necessary technician support handling

required_inputs: supported technician support request type
authoritative work-order, equipment, warranty, part, coverage, authorization, referral, eligibility, and technician evidence
tenant support workflow, service-level expectation, source hierarchy, and closure criteria
refusal_triggers: request type is unsupported in-lane
authoritative workflow status, equipment identity, warranty posture, coverage dependency, or technician note cannot be confirmed
required evidence set is incomplete, stale, or contradictory

required_inputs: audience type, approved channel, and communication purpose
service, warranty, coverage, part, owner, blocker, and next-step state
PHI, consent, minimum-necessary, complaint, and safety communication constraints
required_outputs: safe wording for blockers or next steps
refusal_triggers: communication would disclose excess PHI, imply clinical judgment, approve payer or warranty outcome, or overstate authority
required caveat, source record, owner, or unresolved item is missing
message conflicts with documented policy, payer rule, manufacturer rule, or evidence

required_inputs: unsupported ask details
documented policy, warranty, service-contract, payer, safety, and approval thresholds
exception owner map and evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: request asks for unsupported workaround, discretionary exception, policy override, warranty exception, financial approval, part substitution, safety release, or payer interpretation
approval owner is missing or outside the lane
exception would bypass PHI, clinical, payer, safety, complaint, documentation, or service controls

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
clinical protocol, order, encounter, device, authorization, referral, medical necessity, payer, and care-team authority map
administrative technician support facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical suitability, medical necessity, care-pathway direction, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, or payer coverage interpretation
equipment support includes patient-safety indicators that require licensed review
administrative support would change or imply clinical or payer authority

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient-safety, device malfunction, home-use device, field-service, technician, clinical-risk, and service-risk indicators
privacy, clinical, safety, manufacturer, compliance, technician, or command owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, broad distribution, dealer routing, or manufacturer routing without documented authorization
device malfunction, field-service, technician, clinical, or patient-safety signal requires accountable clinical, safety, manufacturer, privacy, or technician owner
minimum-necessary handling cannot be established
